document: UK Quality Code for Higher Education 2024

conditions.title: Taking a strategic approach to managing quality and standards

criteria.text: Providers demonstrate they have a strategic approach to securing academic standards and assuring and enhancing quality that is embedded across the organisation.
criteria.threshold: Evidence of institution-wide quality strategy with clear ownership and governance
risk_indicators: No documented quality strategy or framework
Quality function not represented at senior leadership level
Quality strategy not referenced in board papers or annual review
Disconnect between quality processes and institutional strategy

conditions.notes: Strategic theme. Foundational principle — all other principles depend on this being in place.

conditions.title: Engaging students as partners

criteria.text: Providers take deliberate steps to engage students as active partners in assuring and enhancing the quality of the student learning experience. Engagement happens individually and collectively to influence all levels of study and decision making.
criteria.threshold: Demonstrable student engagement in quality processes at programme, departmental and institutional level
risk_indicators: Low NSS student voice scores (below 60%)
Student governor absent from board meetings
No student representation on academic committees
Student feedback not demonstrably acted upon or closed the loop

conditions.notes: Strategic theme. Links directly to NSS student voice questions and OfS B2.

conditions.title: Resourcing delivery of a high-quality learning experience

criteria.text: Providers plan, secure and maintain resources relating to learning, technology, facilities and staffing to enable the delivery and enhancement of an accessible, innovative and high-quality learning experience for students that aligns with the provider's strategy and the composition of the student body.
criteria.threshold: Adequate staffing, library, IT and physical resources proportionate to student numbers and programme portfolio
risk_indicators: Staff:student ratio significantly above sector median
High staff vacancy rate or turnover
NSS learning resources scores below 70%
Library or IT investment declining year on year
Capital expenditure below depreciation

conditions.notes: Evaluating quality theme. Links to OfS B2 resources and support condition.

conditions.title: Using data to inform and evaluate quality

criteria.text: Providers collect, analyse and utilise qualitative and quantitative data at provider, departmental, programme and module levels. These analyses inform decision-making with the aim of enhancing practices and processes relating to teaching, learning and the wider student experience.
criteria.threshold: Data infrastructure in place; outcomes data used systematically in quality review processes
risk_indicators: Annual programme reviews not data-driven
No benchmarking against sector or subject-level data
Inconsistent data collection across programmes or campuses
Data not disaggregated by protected characteristics

conditions.notes: Evaluating quality theme. Underpins all quality monitoring activity.

conditions.title: Monitoring, evaluating and enhancing provision

criteria.text: Providers regularly monitor and review their provision to secure academic standards and enhance quality. Deliberate steps are taken to engage and involve students, staff and external expertise in monitoring and evaluation activity.
criteria.threshold: Annual programme review completed for all programmes; periodic review conducted on cycle not exceeding 5 years
risk_indicators: Periodic review overdue (more than 5 years since last review)
Annual programme reviews not completed for all programmes
External examiner concerns not addressed in review cycle
Enhancement actions from previous reviews not implemented

conditions.notes: Evaluating quality theme. Directly links to OfS B1 academic experience condition.

conditions.title: Engaging in external review and accreditation

criteria.text: Providers engage with external reviews to give assurance about the effectiveness of their approach to managing quality and standards. Reviews may be undertaken by representative organisations, agencies or professional, statutory and regulatory bodies (PSRBs).
criteria.threshold: Active engagement with QAA review, OfS monitoring, and relevant PSRB accreditations
risk_indicators: PSRB accreditation lapsed or under review
Previous QAA review findings not addressed
No engagement with relevant subject or professional body reviews
TEF submission not made or rating declined

conditions.notes: Evaluating quality theme. Links to OfS B6 TEF participation and B5 sector-recognised standards.

conditions.title: Designing, developing, approving and modifying programmes

criteria.text: Providers design, develop, approve and modify programmes and modules to ensure the quality of provision and the academic standards of awards are consistent with the relevant Qualifications Framework. Providers ensure their provision and level of qualifications are comparable to those offered across the UK.
criteria.threshold: All programmes approved through documented process; qualifications aligned to FHEQ; curriculum mapping completed
risk_indicators: Programme approval process not documented or applied consistently
Awards not mapped to FHEQ level descriptors
Curriculum not reviewed against subject benchmark statements
Significant programme modifications not subject to approval process

conditions.notes: Implementing quality theme. Links to OfS B4 assessment and awards condition.

conditions.title: Operating partnerships with other organisations

criteria.text: Providers and their partners agree proportionate arrangements for effective governance to secure the academic standards and enhance the quality of programmes and modules that are delivered in partnership with others. Organisations agree and communicate mutual and specific responsibilities.
criteria.threshold: Formal partnership agreements in place; oversight mechanisms documented; partner performance monitored
risk_indicators: Franchise or validation arrangements without formal agreements
Partner institutions not subject to same quality oversight as direct delivery
Subcontractor arrangements not disclosed to OfS
Student outcomes at partner sites diverging significantly from main campus

conditions.notes: Implementing quality theme. Links directly to OfS E10 subcontracting condition.

conditions.title: Recruiting, selecting and admitting students

criteria.text: Providers operate recruitment, selection and admissions processes that are transparent, fair and inclusive. Providers maintain and publish accurate, relevant and accessible information about their provision, enabling students to make informed choices.
criteria.threshold: Admissions policy published; entry requirements clearly stated; information accurate and up to date
risk_indicators: Admissions policy not published or outdated
Course information inaccurate or misleading
Entry requirements inconsistently applied
High conversion drop-off suggesting expectation mismatch

conditions.notes: Implementing quality theme. Links to OfS consumer protection conditions C1, C5.

conditions.title: Supporting students to achieve their potential

criteria.text: Providers facilitate a framework of support for students that enables them to have a high-quality learning experience and achieve their potential as they progress in their studies. The support structure scaffolds the academic, personal and professional learning journey.
criteria.threshold: Academic, pastoral and careers support available and accessible to all students; support needs of diverse student body met
risk_indicators: NSS academic support scores below sector median
Continuation rates significantly below sector benchmark
Attainment gaps between student groups not addressed
Mental health and wellbeing support inadequate or inaccessible

conditions.notes: Implementing quality theme. Links to OfS B2 and student outcomes conditions.

conditions.title: Teaching, learning and assessment

criteria.text: Providers facilitate a collaborative and inclusive approach that enables students to have a high-quality learning experience and to progress through their studies. Assessment employs a variety of methods, embodying the values of academic integrity, producing outcomes that are comparable across the UK and recognised globally.
criteria.threshold: Assessment strategy documented; marking criteria published; external examining in place; academic integrity policy enforced
risk_indicators: External examiner conditions raised and unresolved
NSS assessment and feedback scores below 65%
Grade inflation identified by external examiner
Academic misconduct policy outdated or inconsistently applied
Significant variation in standards across campuses or modes of delivery

conditions.notes: Implementing quality theme. Links to OfS B1, B4 and sector-recognised standards.

conditions.title: Operating concerns, complaints and appeals processes

criteria.text: Providers operate processes for complaints and appeals that are robust, fair, transparent and accessible, and clearly articulated to staff and students. Policies and processes for concerns, complaints and appeals are regularly reviewed and the outcomes are used to support the enhancement of provision.
criteria.threshold: Complaints policy published; OIA scheme membership maintained; complaint volumes and outcomes reported to governance
risk_indicators: High volume of complaints escalated to OIA
OIA upholding rate above sector average
Complaints policy not published or outdated
Complaint themes not feeding into quality improvement
Student Protection Plan not reflecting complaint risks

conditions.notes: Implementing quality theme. Links to OfS C conditions on consumer protection and student protection.
